title: DDoS Attack Mitigation & Back-End XML/SOAP Endpoint Protection

steps.message: High traffic spike detected (100,000 RPS). Block the 500 unique IP addresses identified in the security logs at the edge firewall level.

Review and harden the /bank/27/Default.aspx endpoint. Based on KB findings, implement input validation to prevent SOAP injection and XXE (XML External Entity) attacks that may be contributing to back-end resource exhaustion.

params.description: Disable DTDs and external entities in the XML/SOAP processing logic.

steps.message: Enable Rate Limiting on the API Gateway specifically for SOAP/XML requests to prevent attackers from cycling through IP/port numbers as described in the SSRF/XXE exploitation context.
Approve the activation of the Upstream DDoS Protection Service (e.g., Cloudflare/Akamai) to absorb the 100k RPS load if mitigation steps on-prem fail to stabilize latency.
Incident report initiated for a critical availability event. Notify relevant parties regarding potential service interruption and data integrity check for back-end banking components.
